reforms.description: Latvia plans to boost cooperation in innovation, particularly with foreign institutions, to enhance productivity growth and capture higher value from global value chain participation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing innovation cooperation is expected to drive productivity growth and improve the overall economic performance of Latvia.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to boost cooperation in innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant step towards improving Latvia's innovation landscape and productivity, although it is not a complete overhaul of existing systems.
reforms.source_quote: Innovation is the key driver of productivity growth and essential in capturing higher value added from GVC participation.

reforms.description: Latvia has initiated several policy measures to enhance innovation and cooperation, particularly through the EU-funded Competence Centre support programme aimed at fostering collaboration between research institutions and businesses to develop new products.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating collaboration between research institutions and businesses, this programme enhances the ability of firms to innovate and develop new products, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme is part of ongoing efforts to boost innovation and cooperation in Latvia.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to improve collaboration between research and industry, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity in Latvia.
reforms.source_quote: Latvia has taken several policy initiatives in order to boost innovation and co-operation, especially in areas defined in the Smart Specialisation Strategy.

reforms.description: The Applied Research Grants programme has been established to support research projects that have practical applications, thereby enhancing the link between research and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By funding applied research, this programme aims to translate scientific knowledge into practical solutions, fostering innovation and economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme is part of Latvia's strategy to enhance applied research and its relevance to industry.
reforms.importance_rationale: This programme is a significant step towards aligning research efforts with industry needs, which is essential for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The EU-funded Applied Research Grants programme aims to support research and development activities that lead to commercialization.

reforms.description: The evaluation of the newly reformed tax incentives for R&D is planned for 2017 to assess their effectiveness in reaching young firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Evaluating tax incentives can enhance their effectiveness, potentially leading to increased R&D investment by young firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The evaluation of the R&D tax incentive is planned for 2017.
reforms.importance_rationale: The evaluation of tax incentives is significant as it aims to improve the effectiveness of existing policies, which can have a meaningful impact on R&D activities.
reforms.source_quote: The evaluation of the newly reformed tax incentives for R&D, also in view to reaching young firms, is planned in 2017.

reforms.description: Research institutes have been consolidated through the allocation of funds following an external assessment of research quality, reducing the number of publicly financed research institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Consolidating research institutes can enhance efficiency and focus resources on high-quality research, potentially leading to better research outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: Research institutes have been consolidated through the allocation of funds following an external assessment of research quality.
reforms.importance_rationale: The consolidation of research institutes is a significant reform aimed at improving the efficiency and effectiveness of public research funding.
reforms.source_quote: Research institutes have been consolidated through the allocation of funds following an external assessment of research quality.

reforms.description: The Technology Transfer Program was launched to support the commercialization of public research, including the Innovation Voucher that promotes knowledge transfer from research institutes to SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating knowledge transfer and commercialization, the program aims to enhance innovation and competitiveness among SMEs.
reforms.status_evidence: The new Technology Transfer Program that supports the commercialization of public research was launched in 2016.
reforms.importance_rationale: The program is significant as it directly supports the commercialization of research, which is crucial for enhancing innovation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The new Technology Transfer Program that supports the commercialization of public research was launched in 2016.

reforms.description: The network of vocational education and training (VET) schools was consolidated in 2015, and 18 schools were upgraded to VET Competence Centres. This reform aims to enhance the quality and relevance of vocational training in Latvia.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving vocational education and training enhances the skills of the workforce, which is essential for productivity growth and innovation in firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The network of VET schools was consolidated and upgraded as planned.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in vocational education, aiming to improve the quality of skills training and better align it with labor market needs.
reforms.source_quote: The network of vocational education and training (VET) schools was consolidated in 2015 and 18 schools were upgraded to VET Competence Centres.

reforms.description: A project funded by the EU to expand the provision of workplace-based learning was launched in December 2016, aiming to enhance practical training opportunities for VET students.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding workplace-based learning opportunities increases the practical skills of students, making them more employable and enhancing overall productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: A project funded by the EU to expand the provision of workplace-based learning was launched.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative significantly enhances the practical training aspect of vocational education, which is crucial for aligning skills with labor market demands.
reforms.source_quote: A project funded by the EU to expand the provision of workplace-based learning was launched in December 2016.

reforms.description: Latvia aims to strengthen its innovation capabilities by investing in advanced technologies, skills, and superior management practices to improve its comparative advantage in high-value activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing innovation capabilities is expected to lead to higher productivity and the creation of more high-skilled jobs, which can boost economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative to strengthen innovation capabilities was highlighted as a key policy direction.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards prioritizing innovation in the economy, which is crucial for long-term growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Strengthening innovation capabilities and investing in advanced technologies, skills and superior management practice would improve the comparative advantage in such activities.

reforms.description: Policies that boost skills, innovation, and resource allocation are essential for fostering inclusive participation in global value chains (GVCs) and enhancing competitiveness in knowledge-intensive activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to education and skills development enhances the workforce's capability, which is crucial for innovation and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: Policies aimed at improving skills and innovation have been highlighted as necessary for GVC participation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses a critical need for skilled labor to support innovation and competitiveness, which is significant but not a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Policies that boost skills, innovation and resource allocation foster inclusive participation in GVCs.

reforms.description: Latvia has implemented a new funding system for tertiary education that allocates resources based on quality assessments and alignment with labor market demand, particularly in STEM fields.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning funding with labor market needs and innovation activities, the reform aims to enhance the supply of skilled workers, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The new funding system has been established and is in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how higher education is funded, directly impacting the quality of education and the alignment with labor market needs.
reforms.source_quote: A new funding system allocates resources according to the results of these assessments, the alignment of university programs with labor market demand, and innovation activities by tertiary education institutes.

reforms.description: The government has set an ambitious target to increase adult education participation to 15% by 2020, supported by an action plan that includes governance frameworks and quality control.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing adult education participation enhances the skill set of the workforce, which is essential for adapting to technological changes and improving productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: An action plan that establishes a governance framework and quality control of adult education was approved in 2016.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it aims to improve the skills of the adult population, which is crucial for economic competitiveness, but it is not as transformative as structural reforms.
reforms.source_quote: The government has set an ambitious target to have 15% of the adult population actively involved in education and training by 2020.

reforms.description: Latvia has implemented the EU-funded Competence Centre support programme to enhance the competitiveness of Latvian firms by fostering cooperation between research institutions and businesses in developing new products.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating collaboration between research institutions and firms, the programme is expected to enhance innovation and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been put into action to support innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant effort to enhance innovation through collaboration, although it is part of a broader set of measures.
reforms.source_quote: The EU-funded Competence Centre support programme aims to raise the competitiveness of Latvian firms by fostering cooperation between research institutions in developing new products.

reforms.description: The government plans to increase funding for programs that have demonstrated strong evaluation results, aiming to strengthen innovation capabilities and enhance knowledge transfer.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for effective programs is expected to enhance innovation capabilities, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to increase funding for successful programs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in funding for programs with proven results, which can positively impact innovation and economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: Increase government funding of programmes with strong evaluation results.
